_comment: Human-owned, theme-agnostic plot grab bag. Each entry is a structural beat SHAPE the arc planner adapts to the series' cast and theme — it is a curated catalog (like the sound library and voice registry), not model output. 'fits' lists the arc positions where the shape works: opening (episode 1), finale (last episode), middle (everything between). The model only does thematic specialization; the structure stays human-owned, which also keeps an exotic theme from collapsing the arc.

plots.name: Ordinary world disrupted
plots.shape: Establish the protagonist's normal world and what they want, then introduce the disruption or call that sets the whole series in motion.

plots.name: Thrown together
plots.shape: Force two or more of the cast into an uneasy alliance by a shared danger or goal, sketching the friction that will drive them.

plots.name: A stranger arrives
plots.shape: A newcomer, message, or omen arrives bearing the inciting mystery; the cast must decide whether to trust it.

plots.name: Rising threat
plots.shape: Escalate the central danger a clear notch — raise the stakes or shrink the time the cast has, and show them adapting.

plots.name: Betrayal within
plots.shape: An ally's loyalty is broken or thrown into doubt, fracturing the group's trust at the worst moment.

plots.name: False victory
plots.shape: The cast wins something they wanted, only for the victory to unravel and leave them worse off than before.

plots.name: A secret revealed
plots.shape: A hidden truth about a character or the world surfaces, reframing what the cast thought they understood.

plots.name: Costly bargain
plots.shape: A character trades something they value for an advantage, and the price comes due in a way they did not expect.

plots.name: Outside pressure closes in
plots.shape: An external faction or force tightens around the cast, forcing them to move, hide, or fight on someone else's terms.

plots.name: The group fractures
plots.shape: The cast splits over a hard choice; the disagreement, not the enemy, is the episode's real obstacle.

plots.name: A plan fails
plots.shape: A confident plan collapses; the cast regroups, learns something new, and has to find another way forward.

plots.name: An unlikely ally
plots.shape: Help arrives from an unexpected quarter — a former rival, an outsider, a forgotten resource — at a cost or with strings attached.

plots.name: Final confrontation
plots.shape: Bring the series' central conflict to a head in a direct confrontation the cast can no longer avoid.

plots.name: Sacrifice and resolution
plots.shape: A real cost is paid to resolve the through-line; close the arc and show how the cast and world are changed.

plots.name: A last twist, then close
plots.shape: A final reversal recontextualizes the conflict, then the story resolves — leaving either peace or a deliberate hook.
